commands.review_summary:
The PR threads active model metadata from `createOpenClawTools` into the `image` tool and adds tests for using a vision-capable active model when `agents.defaults.imageModel` is unset.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main has active model metadata at tool construction but does not pass it into image-tool model resolution. I did not run a live provider scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof and normal maintainer review; there is no narrow automated repair needed.

target.title: fix: use active vision model for image tool fallback
target.body:
## Root cause
The `image` tool only treated `agents.defaults.imageModel` as the usable image-capable model. When that setting was empty, even if the active model itself supported vision, attachment images could still fail with `No image model is configured`.

## Fix
- Pass the active model metadata into the image tool.
- Let the image tool use a vision-capable active model before falling back to configured/default image models.
- Keep explicit `agents.defaults.imageModel`, fallback chains, and provider auth behavior intact.

## Verification
- `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.tts-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Coverage
This covers attachment images, inline `data:` images, and remote product images through the same image-tool path.
target.author: marchpure
